news.title: DB Ozone shops got OC despite missing MMRDA NOC, shows RTI
news.para: Residents of DB Ozone housing project face delays in receiving occupation certificates for their homes. Commercial spaces within the same project have obtained occupancy clearance and are operational. This comes as the developer, Neel Kamal Realtors, has outstanding obligations under the state's Rental Housing Scheme. Legal interventions and court orders highlight the ongoing dispute over project completion and compliance.

news.title: Plot-wise ready reckoner rates in Nagpur within two years: Maharashtra government
news.para: Maharashtra's Revenue Minister announced a new micro-zoning system for Ready Reckoner rates, aiming to end uniform valuations within localities. This property-by-property survey, starting in Mumbai, will assign distinct rates based on infrastructure and amenities, differentiating between slum dwellings, standalone homes, and high-rises. The initiative, driven by the CM and public demand, promises fairer property valuations across the state.

news.title: DDA warns architects, engineers & owners against lying about building plans
news.para: Delhi Development Authority (DDA) has issued a stern warning to architects, engineers, and property owners against submitting false information during building plan approvals. This move follows multiple building collapses, with the DDA emphasizing that misrepresentations or concealed facts will lead to serious actions, including blacklisting and legal measures.

news.title: Cement demand growth likely to moderate to around 5% in FY27: Ambuja Cements
news.para: Ambuja Cements anticipates a slowdown in Indian cement demand growth to approximately 5 percent in fiscal year 2026-27. The company is actively expanding its production capacity, aiming for 119 million tonnes per annum by fiscal year 2026-27. This expansion is driven by strategic acquisitions and ongoing project execution to meet future market needs.

news.title: Delhi govt mulls making fire safety measures mandatory in low-rise residential buildings
news.para: Delhi government is considering mandatory fire safety measures like smoke detectors and extinguishers for low-rise residential buildings. This move follows recent fire tragedies that resulted in multiple fatalities. Currently, such buildings are exempt from fire safety certificate requirements.

news.title: Delhi: Poor response to property regularisation portal pushes MCD to plan fee cut, awareness camps
news.para: The MCD is considering reducing property regularisation charges after receiving only two applications on its SWAGAM portal. Officials cite a lack of urgency and immediate incentives for the low uptake among eligible beneficiaries.  Discussions with the DDA are underway to lower fees and encourage more residents to apply.

news.title: Emaar to develop $55 billion real estate project in Dubai: Founder
news.para: Emaar Properties unveiled plans for a colossal $55 billion urban district in Dubai, aiming to house 150,000 residents. This ambitious project, spanning 4.5 million square meters, signifies a strong bet on Dubai's property market resilience despite regional conflict. The development promises iconic views and follows government efforts to bolster investor confidence.

news.title: Temporary migration for work no ground to deny PMAY-Gramin benefits: Govt to CIC
news.para: Migrant labourers won't be denied government housing under PMAY-G simply for working away from their native villages, the Rural Development Ministry clarified.  The ministry informed the Central Information Commission that no rule exists to disqualify eligible families based on temporary relocation for employment. Beneficiary selection relies on SECC 2011 data and local verification.

news.title: Prime Securities group company launches ₹1,000 crore real estate AIF
news.para: Prime Litmus Real Estate Opportunities Fund, a Category II AIF, has a target corpus of ₹750 crore and a green shoe option of ₹250 crore. The fund will invest in structured credit opportunities in under-construction real estate projects across major markets.

news.title: Delhi: Uniform property tax system for NDMC residents likely to be rolled out in coming weeks
news.para: New Delhi Municipal Council (NDMC) is implementing a uniform property tax regime, replacing the decades-old rateable value system with the Unit Area Method (UAM). This change, following amendments to the NDMC Act, 1994, will base tax liability on objective factors like built-up area and location, aiming for greater transparency and reduced disputes.

news.title: Shreyas Iyer leases residential property in Worli, Mumbai for ₹7.14 crore over three years
news.para: Cricketer Shreyas Iyer has secured a luxurious Mumbai residence in Worli's Artesia, agreeing to a substantial monthly rent starting at ₹18.50 lakh. This expansive 3,875 sq ft unit, complete with four parking spots, comes with a three-year lease.  The rental agreement includes a seven percent annual increase, culminating in a total lease value of ₹7.14 crore over the period.

news.title: Oberoi Realty infuses ₹268.50 crore in SPV for Bandra East railway land project
news.para: Centerstage Realty, incorporated on June 2, 2026, is a wholly-owned subsidiary of Oberoi Realty and will undertake the development of about 11 acres of railway land in Bandra East, Mumbai, leased by RLDA for 99 years.

news.title: Kochi metro phase II further delayed: Commissioning pushed to August 2027
news.para: Kochi Metro's Phase II project faces a significant delay. The ambitious plan to connect JLN Stadium to Infopark via Kakkanad will now be commissioned in August 2027. This postponement is attributed to external funding approvals and utility hurdles. Despite challenges, construction is progressing steadily, with foundation and viaduct works nearing completion.

news.title: Greater Bengaluru authority's five corporations collects ₹2,949.56 crore in property tax
news.para: Bengaluru East City Corporation leads property tax collection, achieving 47.35% of its target. Overall, the Greater Bengaluru Authority's five corporations collected `2,949.56 crore, 44.02% of their annual goal. Strong early payments, aided by a 5% rebate, contributed to improved revenue, though North City Corporation saw a decline.

news.title: Vijayawada civic body reviews progress of QR-based digital door numbering project
news.para: Vijayawada Municipal Corporation reviewed its QR code-based digital door numbering project, aiming to assign unique digital identities to properties for enhanced civic services. Officials were directed to ensure accuracy in data collection, mapping, and integration with the ERP system to boost transparency and efficiency.

news.title: Noida Sports City revival helps developer to launch new projects amid constraints in land supply
news.para: Noida is gearing up for a transformation with the revival of its stalled Sports City projects! Thanks to the Supreme Court's recent decision, a massive 20 million sq ft of residential and commercial space will soon be under construction. This development marks a crucial victory for countless homebuyers, with developers collaborating to finalize these projects.

news.title: KIADB to digitise land management process in 100 days, says industries' minister
news.para: Karnataka's Industries Minister M B Patil announced a 100-day deadline for digitizing the entire land management and plot allotment process of the KIADB. This initiative aims to boost transparency, streamline investor services, and enhance record management by creating an Integrated Land Management System for all industrial plots across the state.

news.title: Pimpri Chinchwad: PCMC property tax on common amenities draws flak from housing societies
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad residents are protesting the civic body's decision to levy property tax on amenity spaces like clubhouses and swimming pools. Societies are receiving hefty bills, arguing this is a new financial burden on residents already covering maintenance and essential services. Officials claim it's an effort to tax previously unassessed properties under existing rules.

news.title: Gurugram: Man cons 242 home buyers through allotment fraud, held
news.para: Haryana Police's Economic Offences Wing apprehended a 33-year-old man at Delhi Airport, accused of defrauding individuals by promising flats in a Gurugram housing society. He allegedly collected crores from 242 applicants, failing to deliver homes or refunds. The accused, Anubhav Bhardwaj, was planning to flee the country when arrested.
